texto: Hey, when it comes to jerkbaits, body styles matter. Look at this body style. This is a KVD, a Strike King. Big, wide, fat, a little bit round. This is a Mega Bass. Nice, thin, flat sides. I call this a Twitchbait. Another good version of a Twitchbait. The Rapala Shadow Wrap. Doesn't take a lot of movement. A little bit of hit to get these to really move. They like to stay in place. On the other side, I call these Ripbaits. Look at them. Big, round bodies. These are called Ripbaits because it takes a lot to get them to move. And they move forward toward you. They don't stay in the same place near as much. But something that you can do is put a dress treble. First off, it looks super natural. Second off, it creates a little bit of drag. So you'll get a little more side to side because that feather will drag. So when you need a bait that's going to stay in place, be sure you pay attention to body style.
